---
title: "Week 2 — LLM literacy for bio"
subtitle: "How the tools work, and how to prompt them"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Learning objectives
- Explain, at a working level, how next-token prediction and transformer attention produce LLM behavior.
- Apply at least three reliable prompting patterns and diagnose why a weak prompt fails.
- Distinguish tasks suited to direct chat from tasks suited to an agentic workflow.
- Identify disclosure obligations for AI use in your research context.
:::

## Topics

- How LLMs work, at a working level.
- Prompting patterns that actually help.
- Tool use and agents — a short tour.
- Ethics and limits in a research context.

## Before class

Read:

- [How LLMs work](../literacy/how-llms-work.qmd)
- [Prompting](../literacy/prompting.qmd)
- [Tool use & agents](../literacy/tool-use-and-agents.qmd)
- [Ethics & limits](../literacy/ethics-and-limits.qmd)

## In class

**Short lecture (~20 min):** What is under the hood — next-token prediction, attention, training stages, why fabrication happens. Focus on the "what this means for your workflow" section of [How LLMs work](../literacy/how-llms-work.qmd), not the mechanism.

**Live prompt clinic (~40 min):** Each attendee brings one real prompt that disappointed them. For each prompt:

1. **Diagnose**: which failure mode applies? (Vague request? Missing context? Wrong output format? Asking for recall instead of grounding?)
2. **Fix**: apply one change from the [Prompting](../literacy/prompting.qmd) patterns (role + task + context + format; few-shot; constrained output; etc.).
3. **Rerun live**: compare the new output to the original.
4. **Record**: keep a running log — original prompt / diagnosis / fix / verdict. This log is part of the deliverable.

Prompt clinic works best with prompts from your own domain; generic examples lose the group quickly. Come prepared.

**Demo (~20 min):** A minimal tool-using agent — the bibliography-cleanup agent from [Tool use & agents](../literacy/tool-use-and-agents.qmd). Focus on the human-confirm gate: why it is the critical design decision, not the automation loop.

## After class

- **Deliverable** *(part of weekly exercises, 30%)*:
  1. **Prompt-engineering exercise**: take a recurring task from your work. Produce (a) a weak prompt, (b) a strong prompt using patterns from [Prompting](../literacy/prompting.qmd), (c) outputs from each, (d) a short critique explaining what changed and why.
  2. **Clinic log**: if your prompt was discussed in class, append the diagnosis and fix. If it wasn't, apply the four-step clinic protocol yourself outside class.
  3. **Disclosure statement** following the [rubric](../course-syllabus.qmd#disclosure-rubric). Note which AI tools you used to produce or improve the prompts in parts 1 and 2.

## Supplementary

Slides, recording, and notebooks will be posted here after each session.
